data.title: Hong Kong Ethereum ETF holds nearly 20,000 ETH, up more than 35% in half a year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSo Value data, the total holdings of Hong Kong's three Ethereum spot ETFs are about 19,700 ETH, an increase of more than 35% from six months ago. Among them, China Asset Management (Hong Kong) and Harvest Global's spot Ethereum ETFs, which are virtual asset custody and trading partners of OSL Digital Securities Co., Ltd., hold more than 13,100 ETH, accounting for more than 65%.

data.title: The whale liquidated 6,777 ETH in 24 hours, with a loss of about $1.01 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, in the past 24 hours, a whale liquidated 6777 ETH (about 12.8 million US dollars), with a cumulative loss of about 1.01 million US dollars.
Data shows that the whale withdrew ETH from Binance at an average price of 2038 US dollars through two addresses between August 2023 and September 2024. 2 hours ago, its last 3777 ETH was recharged back to Binance. If all are sold, it will be completely liquidated.

data.title: The total number of GT tokens pledged on the chain increased to 38.65 million, with a static return of 6.80%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The latest on-chain data shows that the total number of GT on-chain pledges has increased to 38.65 million, an increase of 2.26 million from the previous quarter, with a static return of up to 6.80%. It is reported that GT's 24-hour increase reached 3.27%, and is now reported at $20.363.

data.title: Bankrbot disables Grok interactions, halting AI-triggered token creation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Bankrbot development team has adjusted the system to prevent it from responding to Grok's interactions to end the phenomenon of token creation accidentally triggered by AI interactions.
Grok was developed by Elon Musk's xAI, and it had previously inadvertently contributed to the generation of 17 tokens. The incident originated in early March, when X user @coin_domin asked Grok for a suitable token name and suggested that Bankrbot create a token. Bankrbot then automatically deployed the relevant tokens and established a liquidity pool through the Clanker protocol on the Base blockchain.
Because Grok frequently suggested the creation of new tokens, users took advantage of this feature to deploy more than 16 additional tokens in an attempt to profit from the "Grok brand effect." Dune on-chain data shows that wallets associated with Grok have accumulated more than $500,000 in transaction fees, and are expected to break through the million-dollar level soon.
However, because Grok cannot effectively manage wallet funds, Bankrbot founder "Deployer" announced that it has cut off Grok's automatic response mechanism and no longer allows it to trigger token deployment to prevent potential risks.

data.title: OKX launches "Ouyi Protect" to fully disclose product security mechanisms
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, OKX has now officially launched "Ouyi Protect", fully disclosing the product security mechanism. The page publicizes OKX's systematic product security mechanisms such as multi-party audits, AI monitoring, POR, self-custody, 24/7 expert support, regulatory compliance, network defense team and vulnerability reward program. At the same time, common safety tips and fraud cases are also provided to further strengthen user safety education. The launch of "Ouyi Protect" not only enhances the platform's self-supervision, but also promotes the healthy development of the industry and a new level of security.

data.title: DeFiance Capital CEO: The crypto infrastructure speculation bubble has burst, and application valuations are more attractive
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Arthur Cheong, founder and CEO of DeFiance Capital, said that the impact of the "Fat Protocol Thesis" in the crypto market is fading, and the speculative bubble of infrastructure projects has burst.
He pointed out that the price-to-earnings ratio (PE) of DeFi applications is usually between 5-15 times, such as Hyperliquid (7.7x), Ena (7.4x) and Jupiter (3.9x), while infrastructure projects such as Ethereum (694x) and Solana (162x) have had limited growth in the past two years, and valuations are still in the 150x-1000x price/revenue ratio range.
Cheong believes that as the market re-evaluates valuations, DeFi and other application-based projects will receive more attention, and the premium of infrastructure projects is being digested by the market.

data.title: Crypto Market May Be Nearing End Of Fat Protocol Theory
data.description: According to PANews, Arthur, the founder and Chief Investment Officer of DeFiance Capital, expressed on social media that the cryptocurrency market might be approaching the end phase of the 'Fat Protocol Theory.' This theory has reportedly caused long-term harm to investments in cryptocurrencies other than Bitcoin. Arthur highlighted that successful application projects are typically valued at five to fifteen times their revenue, whereas infrastructure projects, which have seen little growth over the past two years, are still valued at 150 to 1000 times their revenue. He believes that the speculative premium bubble in crypto infrastructure has officially burst.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 50 Million USDC on Ethereum
data.description: According to BlockBeats, WhaleAlert has reported that the USDC Treasury minted 50 million USDC on the Ethereum blockchain just one minute ago.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission responds to fake Binance SMS scam: no hesitation to take action against any violations, but no comment on individual cases
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Recently, there have been many fake Binance SMS scams in Hong Kong. The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission responded that according to the Securities and Futures Ordinance and the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance, if a business that operates regulated activities and provides virtual asset services in Hong Kong without a license and/or actively promotes such services to Hong Kong investors in Hong Kong or from outside Hong Kong, it is a crime. The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission will not hesitate to take appropriate action against any violations, but will not comment on individual incidents. Investors are reminded to only buy and sell virtual assets on virtual asset trading platforms licensed by the Securities and Futures Commission. (Hong Kong Wen Wei Po)
